home.kvittTooltip: Swedish for "even", as in all settled up

hero.headline: Split expenses with friends. Stay
hero.subheadline: Kvitt makes it easy to track shared costs, see who owes what, and settle up — all in one place.

howItWorks.headline: Split any expense in three steps
howItWorks.step1Body: Log a shared cost, pick who was involved, and choose how to split it.
howItWorks.step2Body: See who owes what at a glance. Balances update as expenses are added.
howItWorks.step3Body: Record a payment to clear a debt and keep everyone's balance accurate.

cta.headline: Ready to split your first expense?
cta.body: Create a group, invite your friends, and start tracking.

groups.title: Your groups
groups.emptyBody: Create a group to start splitting expenses.

noExpenses.title: No expenses yet
noExpenses.desc: Add the first expense to get started.

balance.settledDesc: No outstanding balances in this group.
balance.youDontOweDesc: Nothing you need to do right now.

expense.dialogDesc: Record a new expense for this group.
expense.noMembersError: Select at least one member for the split
expense.deleteDesc: This will permanently delete "{title}" and all its splits.

createGroup.dialogDesc: Give your group a name. You can invite others after it's created.

settleUp.dialogDesc: Record a payment between two group members.
settleUp.removeDesc: This will remove the recorded settlement between {payer} and {payee}.

groupSettings.regenerateDesc: The old link will stop working. Anyone who hasn't joined yet will need the new link.
groupSettings.deleteGroupDesc: This will permanently delete the group and all its expenses and settlements. This cannot be undone.

profile.title: Profile
profile.dangerDesc: Permanently delete your account. Your name will be replaced with "Deleted user" in all groups, but expense history will be preserved.
profile.deleteDialogDesc: This will permanently delete your account. Your expenses will remain but your name will show as 'Deleted user'. This cannot be undone.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.whatWeCollectBody: We collect your name, email address, and the data you create in the app — groups, expenses, and settlements. We also collect basic usage data to keep the service running.
privacy.howWeUseItBody: Your data is used solely to provide the Kvitt service. We don't sell it, share it with advertisers, or use it for any purpose beyond operating the app.
privacy.dataStorageBody: Data is stored securely in the cloud. We take reasonable technical measures to protect it, but no system is perfectly secure.
privacy.yourRightsBody: You can request deletion of your account and all associated data at any time by contacting us.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.acceptanceBody: By using Kvitt, you agree to these terms. If you don't agree, please don't use the service.
terms.theServiceBody: Kvitt is a tool for tracking shared expenses. It's provided as-is, free of charge. We may change or discontinue it at any time.
terms.yourAccountBody: You're responsible for keeping your account secure and for all activity under it. Don't use Kvitt for anything illegal or harmful to others.
terms.liabilityBody: Kvitt is provided without warranties of any kind. We're not liable for any damages arising from your use of the service, including data loss or financial disputes between users.
terms.changesBody: We may update these terms occasionally. Continued use of Kvitt after changes means you accept the new terms.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.subtitle: New updates and improvements to Kvitt.

entries.title: English & Swedish: full i18n
description: The app is now fully internationalised with English and Swedish support.
A language switcher with flag icons appears in the header. Your preference is saved to your account.
All UI strings, form labels, error messages, categories, and page content have been translated.
Date and number formatting are now locale-aware throughout the app.

entries.title: Home screen support & Google sign-in fixes
description: Kvitt can now be saved to your phone's home screen and opens as a standalone app with no browser chrome.
Added a web app manifest, app icons, and theme color support for a native-feeling experience on both iOS and Android.
Fixed Google OAuth flow so that signing in or signing up with Google now correctly establishes a session.
New users signing in with Google are automatically registered, no need to sign up separately.

entries.title: Bug fixes
description: Fixed the "Add expense" button not appearing in groups with no expenses on mobile.
Fixed a layout scaling issue on mobile caused by a missing viewport meta tag.

entries.title: Initial release
description: Kvitt is live! Track and split shared expenses with friends and groups.
